# pfsense_interface_bridge (Resource)

[Bridge interface](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/interfaces/bridges.html) joining two or more interfaces together at layer 2.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Simple bridge joining two interfaces at layer 2
resource "pfsense_interface_bridge" "lan_bridge" {
  members     = ["lan", "opt1"]
  description = "LAN bridge"
}

# Bridge with Rapid Spanning Tree enabled
resource "pfsense_interface_bridge" "stp_bridge" {
  members        = ["opt1", "opt2"]
  description    = "Bridge with RSTP"
  enable_stp     = true
  protocol       = "rstp"
  priority       = 32768
  stp_interfaces = ["opt1", "opt2"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `members` (List of String) List of logical interface names (e.g. 'lan', 'opt1') that are members of the bridge. At least one is required.

### Optional

- `auto_edge_interfaces` (List of String) Member interfaces that automatically detect edge status. Must be a subset of members.
- `auto_ptp_interfaces` (List of String) Member interfaces that automatically detect point-to-point status. Must be a subset of members.
- `cache_expire` (Number) Time in seconds before bridge address cache entries are expired, between 0 and 3600.
- `description` (String) For administrative reference (not parsed).
- `edge_interfaces` (List of String) Member interfaces treated as edge ports, connecting directly to end stations and unable to create bridging loops. Must be a subset of members.
- `enable_stp` (Boolean) Enable Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) on the bridge.
- `forward_delay` (Number) Time in seconds that must pass before an interface begins forwarding packets when Spanning Tree is enabled, between 4 and 30.
- `hello_time` (Number) Time in seconds between broadcasting of Spanning Tree hello messages (RSTP only ignores this), between 1 and 2.
- `hold_count` (Number) Transmit hold count for Spanning Tree, the number of packets transmitted before being rate limited, between 1 and 10.
- `ip6_link_local` (Boolean) Enable link-local IPv6 addressing on the bridge interface.
- `max_addresses` (Number) Maximum number of bridge address cache entries.
- `max_age` (Number) Maximum age in seconds of a Spanning Tree protocol message, between 6 and 40.
- `priority` (Number) Bridge priority for Spanning Tree, between 0 and 61440 in increments of 4096.
- `private_interfaces` (List of String) Member interfaces marked private, which cannot communicate with any other private port. Must be a subset of members.
- `protocol` (String) Spanning Tree protocol version. Options: `rstp` (Rapid Spanning Tree), `stp` (legacy Spanning Tree).
- `ptp_interfaces` (List of String) Member interfaces treated as point-to-point links. Must be a subset of members.
- `span_interfaces` (List of String) Member interfaces used as span ports, transmitting a copy of every frame received by the bridge. Span ports must NOT be bridge members.
- `static_interfaces` (List of String) Member interfaces with a static (sticky) address cache entry. Must be a subset of members.
- `stp_interfaces` (List of String) Member interfaces on which Spanning Tree is enabled. Must be a subset of members.

### Read-Only

- `bridge_if` (String) Interface name automatically assigned by pfSense to the bridge (e.g. 'bridge0'). Used as the unique identifier.

## Import

Import is supported using the assigned bridge interface name:

```shell
terraform import pfsense_interface_bridge.example bridge0
```
